  • Johns Hopkins faculty
  • Leading AI governance experts
  • Frontier AI researchers
  • Policymakers
  • Government officials
  • Civil society leaders
  • Technology practitioners

3. Fully Funded Participation

The fellowship covers:

  • Travel to Washington, DC
  • Accommodation
  • Meals
  • Incidental expenses

This ensures fellows can participate fully without financial barriers.

4. Cross-Sector Networking

Participants will build relationships with professionals from:

  • Government
  • Academia
  • Civil society
  • Frontier AI laboratories
  • Technology companies
  • Policy organizations
  • Research institutions

5. Collaborative Project Work

The fellowship concludes with group projects aimed at producing meaningful contributions to AGI governance.

Topics Covered During the Fellowship

The curriculum includes deep dives into critical governance questions such as:

Frontier AI Legislation

Participants will analyze:

  • Federal AI legislation
  • State-level AI regulations
  • Emerging global governance frameworks

Democratic AI Alignment

Fellows will explore:

  • Public participation in AI governance
  • Democratic accountability
  • Governance legitimacy
  • Civic engagement in AI decision-making

Building Societal Resilience

Topics include:

  • Economic disruption
  • Labor market transitions
  • Institutional adaptation
  • Social stability amid AI transformation

New Governance Institutions

Participants will investigate:

  • Future regulatory bodies
  • Governance mechanisms for AGI
  • Institutional innovation
  • Cross-border governance structures

Human Identity and AGI

The fellowship also examines philosophical and societal implications of advanced AI systems.

Illustrative Fellowship Projects

Participants may work on initiatives such as:

  • Civic AI agents supporting democratic decision-making
  • Model AGI legislation
  • Frontier AI evaluations
  • Governance tools for advanced AI systems
  • Public-interest AI governance frameworks
  • Research on recursive self-improvement risks
  • AI-enabled governance systems

Fellowship Structure

Duration

The fellowship runs for three weeks.

Dates

September 8 – September 25, 2026

Location

Johns Hopkins Bloomberg Center

Washington, DC, USA

Format

The fellowship is conducted entirely in person.

Weekly Activities

Participants should expect:

  • Faculty lectures
  • Interactive seminars
  • Collaborative workshops
  • Guest speaker sessions
  • Group projects
  • Policy discussions
  • Research activities

Fellowship Expectations

This is an intensive full-time program.

Participants are expected to:

  • Complete significant pre-reading
  • Attend all sessions
  • Engage actively in discussions
  • Collaborate on projects
  • Participate throughout the entire program

The fellowship is intentionally demanding and designed for individuals serious about shaping the future of AI governance.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship seeks ambitious early-career researchers and practitioners who already possess foundational expertise in AI governance.

Potential applicants include:

  • Congressional staffers
  • Policy professionals
  • AI company employees
  • Researchers
  • Academics
  • Grantmakers
  • Nonprofit leaders
  • Governance specialists
  • Public policy professionals
  • Computer scientists
  • Technology experts

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

Academic Qualifications

Have either:

  • A postgraduate degree in a relevant field, OR
  • Equivalent practical experience

Career Stage

Be early career professionals, generally:

  • Within eight years of receiving an undergraduate degree

Knowledge Requirements

Applicants should already possess:

  • Understanding of frontier AI systems
  • Knowledge of AI governance
  • Technical AI knowledge
  • Policy expertise related to AI

The organizers emphasize that this is not a beginner-level transition program but rather an advanced leadership fellowship.

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship stands out because it:

  • Is fully funded
  • Provides direct access to world-class experts
  • Focuses on one of the most important governance challenges of the century
  • Connects fellows with leaders across sectors
  • Offers intensive hands-on learning
  • Builds long-term professional networks
  • Creates opportunities to contribute meaningfully to AGI governance

Application Process

Interested candidates should:

  1. Review eligibility requirements.
  2. Prepare application materials.
  3. Demonstrate relevant experience in AI governance or frontier AI.
  4. Highlight leadership potential and commitment to responsible AI development.
  5. Submit an application through the Official Johns Hopkins AGI Governance Fellowship portal.

Visit HERE to learn more about the Johns Hopkins AGI Governance Fellowship 2026 in Washington.

Final Thoughts

The Johns Hopkins AGI Governance Fellowship 2026 represents one of the most exciting opportunities available to emerging leaders working at the intersection of artificial intelligence, governance, public policy, and societal transformation.

As AI systems become increasingly powerful, societies around the world will need talented professionals capable of designing governance frameworks, institutions, and policies that ensure these technologies benefit humanity. This fellowship offers a rare opportunity to learn from leading experts, collaborate with high-caliber peers, and contribute to shaping the future of AGI governance.

For researchers, policymakers, technologists, and governance professionals who want to play a meaningful role in navigating the AI transition, this fully funded fellowship in Washington, DC could be a career-defining opportunity.

Discover more global Fellowship and AI opportunities on OFY.

The post Johns Hopkins AGI Governance Fellowship 2026 in Washington, DC: Fully Funded Elite AI Governance Program for Emerging Global Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: USIU-Africa Financial Aid and Scholarships 2026: Comprehensive Funding Opportunities for Kenyan and International Students
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Pursuing higher education can be a significant financial investment, but the United States International University-Africa (USIU-Africa) is committed to ensuring that talented and deserving students have access to quality education regardless of their financial circumstances. Through an extensive range of scholarships, grants, bursaries, and financial aid programs, USIU-Africa continues to support students from Kenya, Africa, […]

The post USIU-Africa Financial Aid and Scholarships 2026: Comprehensive Funding Opportunities for Kenyan and International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Pursuing higher education can be a significant financial investment, but the United States International University-Africa (USIU-Africa) is committed to ensuring that talented and deserving students have access to quality education regardless of their financial circumstances. Through an extensive range of scholarships, grants, bursaries, and financial aid programs, USIU-Africa continues to support students from Kenya, Africa, and around the world.

Located in Nairobi, Kenya, USIU-Africa is one of the continent’s leading private universities, offering internationally recognized programs and attracting students from over 70 countries. To make higher education more accessible, the institution provides several funding opportunities designed to reward academic excellence, leadership, community engagement, and financial need.

Why Choose USIU-Africa?

USIU-Africa is renowned for:

  • Internationally accredited degree programs.
  • Diverse student population representing more than 70 countries.
  • Strong emphasis on leadership and innovation.
  • World-class faculty and facilities.
  • Extensive partnerships with universities and organizations worldwide.
  • Commitment to promoting access to quality higher education.

Recognizing that finances can be a barrier to education, the university has established numerous financial aid initiatives to support students throughout their academic journey.

Types of Financial Aid Available at USIU-Africa

USIU-Africa offers various forms of financial assistance, including:

  1. Scholarships
  2. Grants
  3. Bursaries
  4. Tuition assistance programs
  5. Donor-funded awards
  6. Need-based financial aid
  7. Merit-based scholarships

These opportunities are intended to help students pursue their studies without excessive financial burdens.

Merit-Based Scholarships

Students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance may qualify for merit-based scholarships.

These awards are designed to:

  • Recognize academic excellence.
  • Encourage high achievement.
  • Reward outstanding performance.
  • Support talented students pursuing undergraduate and graduate studies.

Recipients are often required to maintain strong academic standing throughout their studies.

Need-Based Financial Aid

USIU-Africa understands that many capable students face economic challenges. Through need-based financial assistance, the university seeks to ensure that financial constraints do not prevent deserving students from obtaining a quality education.

Need-based aid considers:

  • Family income levels.
  • Financial circumstances.
  • Educational expenses.
  • Ability to pay tuition fees.

Students applying for this form of assistance may be required to provide supporting financial documents.

Donor-Supported Scholarships

Several organizations and partners collaborate with USIU-Africa to provide scholarship opportunities for deserving students.

These programs may support:

  • Women in leadership.
  • Refugees and displaced students.
  • Students from marginalized communities.
  • Students pursuing particular academic disciplines.
  • Community development initiatives.

Each donor-funded scholarship has its own eligibility criteria and application procedures.

Scholarships for Refugees and Vulnerable Students

USIU-Africa has become widely recognized for supporting refugees and displaced populations through educational opportunities.

These initiatives aim to:

  • Promote inclusion.
  • Increase access to higher education.
  • Empower disadvantaged communities.
  • Develop future leaders capable of transforming society.

Students benefiting from such programs often receive comprehensive support that extends beyond tuition assistance.

Leadership and Community Impact Scholarships

The university values leadership and social responsibility. Some scholarships are awarded to students who have demonstrated:

  • Leadership potential.
  • Volunteerism.
  • Community service.
  • Social impact initiatives.
  • Entrepreneurial activities.

Such programs seek to nurture future leaders who will contribute positively to society.

Sports Scholarships

Talented athletes may also qualify for sports-related scholarships.

These scholarships help students:

  • Balance academics with athletics.
  • Represent the university in competitions.
  • Continue developing their sporting talents.

Sports scholarships recognize excellence in various disciplines and encourage holistic development.

International Student Support

USIU-Africa attracts students from across Africa and beyond. International students can access several forms of financial aid depending on:

  • Academic merit.
  • Country of origin.
  • Financial need.
  • Sponsorship agreements.
  • Donor requirements.

This commitment strengthens the university’s multicultural learning environment.

Benefits of USIU-Africa Financial Aid

Students who receive financial assistance enjoy numerous advantages, including:

Reduced Financial Burden

Scholarships and grants help offset tuition costs and other educational expenses.

Access to Quality Education

Recipients gain access to internationally accredited programs and world-class facilities.

Leadership Development

Many programs encourage leadership and community engagement.

Career Advancement Opportunities

Students graduate with qualifications recognized globally, increasing employability and career prospects.

Diverse Learning Environment

Scholars become part of a multicultural academic community with students from more than 70 countries.

Application Process

Students interested in financial aid should:

  1. Apply for admission to USIU-Africa.
  2. Review available scholarships and funding opportunities.
  3. Confirm eligibility requirements.
  4. Prepare supporting documents.
  5. Complete the appropriate financial aid application forms.
  6. Submit all required materials within specified deadlines.

Because deadlines vary by scholarship, applicants are advised to regularly monitor the official USIU-Africa Financial Aid webpage for updates and announcements.

Why USIU-Africa Scholarships Matter

Access to education transforms lives and communities. Through its robust financial aid programs, USIU-Africa demonstrates its commitment to inclusivity, academic excellence, and social impact.

By investing in talented students regardless of economic background, the university is helping shape future leaders, innovators, researchers, and professionals who will contribute to Africa’s development and the global community.

Prospective students are encouraged to explore the various scholarships and funding opportunities available and begin their journey toward earning a world-class education at one of Africa’s premier universities.

For complete details and current opportunities, visit the official USIU-Africa Financial Aid page.

Discover global

The post USIU-Africa Financial Aid and Scholarships 2026: Comprehensive Funding Opportunities for Kenyan and International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
